Dashboard Archive: 2026-06-01 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum-ranker data shows two exceptionally strong performers at the top: VPG ranks #1 with a composite score of 96.3, driven by extraordinary consistency across timeframes—it's up 382% over the past year with ratio passes of 4/5 and a near-perfect bad_spy_score of 99.7, meaning it held up exceptionally well on down market days. BAND follows closely with similar year-to-date strength (392% return) and perfect SMA alignment, though its ratio consistency is slightly lower at 3/5 passes, and it shows much higher volatility relative to market moves (bad_spy_score only 1.4). Both securities clear all moving average gates (SMA30/50/100/200), indicating they're trading well above intermediate support levels. The key distinction is momentum durability: VPG's ratios remain steady across longer periods (ratio_6m_3m at 1.73, ratio_1y_3m at 2.44), suggesting sustained strength, while BAND's ratios flatten significantly in the 6-12 month window (1.08 and 1.06), suggesting the bulk of its gain compressed into an earlier period. These top two candidates represent the market's most consistent short-term momentum plays as of today's close, though investors should note VPG's more stable risk
